title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You have read an article in a magazine arguing that teenagers' heavy use of smartphones is harmless. Write a letter to the magazine editor disagreeing with this view, explaining the potential negative effects of smartphone dependence on young people's health, relationships, and academic performance.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Editor,

I am writing in response to your recent article claiming that teenagers' smartphone use is essentially harmless. While I understand the author's perspective, I believe this view significantly underestimates the risks involved.

Research increasingly links excessive smartphone use among adolescents to poor sleep quality, reduced attention spans, and heightened levels of anxiety and depression. The constant availability of social media creates unhealthy social comparisons and can expose young people to cyberbullying, with genuinely serious consequences for mental health.

Academically, the impact is equally concerning. Studies have shown that even the mere presence of a smartphone on a desk can reduce cognitive capacity during tasks requiring concentration. When combined with the addictive design of many apps, it is unsurprising that many teenagers struggle to focus on their studies.

I am not suggesting smartphones should be banned — they are clearly useful tools. However, there is an urgent need for better digital literacy education and guidelines around healthy usage.

I hope your publication will revisit this issue with the seriousness it deserves.

Yours faithfully,
A Concerned Parent

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your school has asked you to write a report on students' use of gadgets and technology, based on a recent survey. Include findings about how students use technology, any negative effects they have noticed, and recommendations for promoting healthier technology habits among young people.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Students' Gadget Use and Its Effects at Riverside Academy

Introduction
This report is based on a survey completed by 200 students aged 13–17, examining their use of technology and any self-reported effects on their wellbeing and academic performance.

Current Technology Use
Survey results indicate that the average student spends approximately six hours per day on screens outside school hours. Smartphones are used most frequently, primarily for social media, gaming, and video streaming. Only 15% of respondents reported using technology primarily for educational purposes during leisure time.

Negative Effects Reported
Over 60% of students admitted that technology use negatively affected their sleep, and nearly half reported difficulty concentrating on homework due to digital distractions. A concerning 30% felt that social media made them feel worse about themselves.

Recommendations
The school should introduce regular digital wellness workshops covering topics such as screen time management and online safety. Additionally, a voluntary 'tech-free hour' initiative before bedtime could be promoted to parents and students alike. Teachers are also encouraged to model healthy technology boundaries within lessons.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Chris has been struggling to manage their time effectively and has asked for advice on useful apps or technology tools that could help. Write an email to Chris recommending a specific app or tech tool, explaining what it does and why you think it would be helpful for them.

Write your email to Chris.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Chris,

I was so glad you asked — I actually had exactly the same problem a few months ago and discovered an app that genuinely transformed how I manage my time. It is called Todoist, and I cannot recommend it highly enough.

Essentially, Todoist is a task management app that lets you create to-do lists, set priorities, and assign deadlines to everything you need to do. What makes it particularly powerful is the way it breaks larger projects into manageable sub-tasks, so overwhelming workloads suddenly feel much more achievable. You can also set recurring reminders for regular commitments, which means nothing ever gets forgotten.

The interface is clean and intuitive, and it syncs seamlessly across your phone, tablet, and laptop, so your task list is always with you. There is a free version that covers most basic needs, though the premium version is very affordable and adds useful features like productivity tracking.

I genuinely think it would make a real difference to your daily routine. Give the free version a try first and see what you think!

Let me know how you get on.

Best,
Alex
